about.description: A ripper mobile app for intervals.icu. Suss out your activities, check your training load, and track your fitness, all from your phone.
about.disclaimer: Veloq is an independent, open-source project. We're not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to intervals.icu in any way. intervals.icu is a trademark of its respective owner.
about.garminNote: Activity data may include info from Garmin devices. Garmin and the Garmin logo are trademarks of Garmin Ltd. or its subsidiaries.
about.mapAttribution: Map tiles are provided by the legends at OpenStreetMap under the Open Database License. Map data © OpenStreetMap contributors.
about.title: About & Legal bits

activity.chartHint: Hold to scrub • Hold chip for axis

explanations.conditions: Temperature from the weather or your gadget's sensor.
explanations.energy: Roughly how many kilojoules you burned based on heart rate, power, and how long you went.
explanations.heartRate: Your average ticker rate during the activity.
explanations.trainingLoad: Training Stress Score (TSS) shows how hard you went based on duration and intensity relative to your threshold.
explanations.yourForm: Your Form (TSB) on this day. It's your overall training form, not just this activity. TSB = Fitness (CTL) minus Fatigue (ATL).

activityDetail.noMatchedSectionsDescription: Sections matching this activity will rock up here
activityDetail.noRouteMatchDescription: Do this route a few more times to start tracking your performance, or expand your timeline in Settings to include older activities.
activityDetail.bodyShapeRandom: Body chosen at random — no gender in intervals.icu profile

alerts.cacheCorruptionMessage: Local data stash got munted and had to be cleared. Your data will be grabbed fresh from the server.
alerts.clearCacheMessage: This'll clear all your cached data, tracks, and route matching stuff, then reload. Might take a few minutes.
alerts.clearRouteCacheMessage: This'll clear all route matching data and get straight into re-analysing tracks.
alerts.disconnectMessage: This'll chuck your API credentials. You'll need to enter them again to use the app.
alerts.failedToClear: Couldn't clear the cache. Have another crack.
alerts.redetectSectionsBusy: Section crunching's already on the go. Hang tight.
alerts.redetectSectionsMessage: Re-run section detection across every activity. Takes a minute or two on bigger libraries.
alerts.syncAllMessage: This'll sync up to 10 years of activity data in the background. Gives you better route analysis and comparison.

attribution.demoData: Demo mode uses real activity traces. Used with permission from the author.
attribution.garminTrademark: Garmin and the Garmin logo are trademarks of Garmin Ltd.

backup.nothingToRestore: Nothing in that backup file, mate.
backup.autoBackupDescription: Daily after sync. Local: keeps last 5. Cloud: keeps all.

backup.backupFailedMessage: Could not complete the backup. Check your backup destination and try again.
backup.legacyImportNotice: This is a legacy backup (.veloq). Only preferences, section names, and route names were restored. For a full restore, use a database backup (.veloqdb).
backup.notEncryptedWarning: Backups are not encrypted. Anyone with access to your backup files can read your training data.

backup.cameraPermissionNeeded: Camera access is required to scan QR codes

fitnessScreen.fatigueDescription: is a 7-day rolling average of how hard you've been going.
fitnessScreen.fitnessDescription: is a 42-day rolling average of how hard you've been going.
fitnessScreen.formDescription: is fitness minus how cooked you are. The
fitnessScreen.title: Fitness & Form

guidance.highRisk: TSB below -30. Fatigue's well and truly stacked up past recent fitness gains.
guidance.optimal: TSB between -30 and -10. Training load is producing fitness adaptations.
guidance.greyZone: TSB between -10 and 5. Not fresh, not cooked — somewhere in between.
guidance.fresh: TSB between 5 and 25. Low fatigue relative to fitness. Hang here too long and fitness starts dropping off.
guidance.transition: TSB above 25. Not much training stimulus lately. Fitness is dropping off.

insights.title: Insights
insights.noInsights: Insights will appear as you train
insights.noInsightsHint: Complete more activities to see personalised insights
insights.loadBody: TSS: {{currentTss}} this week vs {{previousTss}} last week. Duration: {{currentDuration}} vs {{previousDuration}}.
insights.volumeBody: Training duration: {{current}} this week vs {{previous}} last week. This measures time, not training load (TSS).

hrvTrend.trendingUpBody: Your 7-day HRV average ({{avg}} ms) is rising based on {{days}} days of data.
hrvTrend.trendingDownBody: Your 7-day HRV average ({{avg}} ms) is declining based on {{days}} days of data.
hrvTrend.stableBody: Your 7-day HRV average ({{avg}} ms) is within normal range based on {{days}} days of data.

weeklyLoad.title: Training load {{percent}}% {{direction}} average
weeklyLoad.body: This week: {{acute}} TSS vs 4-week average of {{chronic}} TSS ({{percent}}% difference).

stalePr.title: {{section}}: fitness changed since PR
stalePr.subtitle: PR {{prTime}} at {{metric}} {{previous}}, now {{current}} (+{{gainPercent}}%)
stalePr.methodology: Compares fitness metrics at PR time vs current values. Shows sections where fitness metrics have changed since the PR was set.

restDay.tomorrowPatternBody: Based on {{count}} previous activities for this day of the week.

methodology.banister: Calculates Training Stress Balance from the difference between fitness (CTL) and fatigue (ATL).
methodology.hrv: Compares your heart rate variability trend against your 7-day baseline to assess recovery status.
methodology.kmeans: Groups your activities by day, duration, and sport type to identify recurring training patterns.
methodology.prDetection: Compares your latest section time against all previous efforts to detect personal records.
methodology.sectionTrend: Tracks median performance on frequently visited sections over time.
methodology.periodComparison: Compares training metrics between consecutive weeks to track progression.
methodology.hrvDescription: HRV trend based on your 7-day rolling average. HRV accuracy depends on measurement device and consistency. Trends over days are more reliable than single readings.
methodology.periodComparisonRestDay: Compares last week against your 4-week average when the current week has no activities yet.
methodology.ftpEstimation: Tracks changes in your estimated FTP over time based on power data from your activities. FTP detection uses intervals.icu's algorithms, which may include auto-detection from activity data.
methodology.thresholdSpeedDescription: Compares your latest threshold-speed estimate against previous values, then formats the change as athlete-readable pace for display.

insights.disclaimer: Training metrics are estimates based on published exercise science. Individual responses vary. Not medical or coaching advice.
insights.aboutInsightsBody: Training metrics are estimates based on published exercise science models. Individual responses vary significantly. These insights are informational only — not medical or coaching advice.
insights.aboutBody: Training metrics are estimates based on published exercise science models. Individual responses vary significantly. These insights are informational only — not medical or coaching advice.

efficiencyTrend.title: HR trending down on {{name}}
efficiencyTrend.subtitle: {{hrChange}} bpm lower over {{efforts}} efforts
efficiencyTrend.body: Over {{efforts}} efforts on {{name}}, your heart rate at the same pace has dropped by {{hrChange}} bpm.

strengthBalance.noRecentVolume: No volume in the last 4 weeks — only appearing in the wider selected period.
strengthBalance.volumeAppeared: Volume appeared in the last two weeks after a quieter start.
strengthBalance.volumeUp: Recent 2-week average is {{percent}}% above the prior 2 weeks.
strengthBalance.volumeDown: Recent 2-week average is {{percent}}% below the prior 2 weeks.
strengthBalance.volumeSteady: Recent 2-week average is holding steady versus the earlier part of the month.

sectionPr.changed: A repeatable route now carries a new best.
sectionPr.matters: Compares this effort against all previous times on the same section.
sectionPr.next: Route detail stores the effort list and timing.

hrvTrend.changed: Short-term recovery readings nudged up or down.
hrvTrend.matters: Shows the direction of your 7-day HRV rolling average.
hrvTrend.next: Fitness tab shows HRV alongside other wellness data.

periodComparison.changed: Weekly load landed differently than the week before.
periodComparison.matters: Compares this week's load against the previous week.
periodComparison.next: Routes tab lists the activities from both weeks.

fitnessMilestone.changedSwimPace: Your threshold swim speed moved upward, which shows up as faster pace per 100m.
fitnessMilestone.changedRunPace: Your running threshold speed moved upward, which shows up as faster pace per kilometre.
fitnessMilestone.mattersFtp: Tracks the difference between your latest and previous FTP estimates.
fitnessMilestone.mattersPace: Tracks the difference between your latest and previous threshold pace estimates.
fitnessMilestone.nextFtp: Fitness tab shows the FTP trend over time.
fitnessMilestone.nextPace: Fitness tab shows the pace trend over time.

strengthProgression.changed: A muscle group's weighted-set volume moved compared to earlier weeks.
strengthProgression.matters: Compares weighted-set volume in the recent 2 weeks against the earlier 2 weeks.
strengthProgression.next: Strength tab shows the 4-week history per muscle group.

strengthBalance.changed: An antagonist pair shows one side carrying more weighted sets.
strengthBalance.matters: Shows the weighted-set ratio between antagonist muscle pairs over 4 weeks.
strengthBalance.next: Strength tab shows the pair totals side by side.

stalePr.changedPowerGrouped: Current cycling power now sits above the level tied to several section bests.
stalePr.changedPowerSingle: Current cycling power now sits above the level tied to this section best.
stalePr.changedSwimGrouped: Current swim threshold now sits above the level tied to several section bests.
stalePr.changedSwimSingle: Current swim threshold now sits above the level tied to this section best.
stalePr.changedRunGrouped: Current running threshold now sits above the level tied to several section bests.
stalePr.changedRunSingle: Current running threshold now sits above the level tied to this section best.
stalePr.changedGenericGrouped: Current fitness now sits above the level tied to several section bests.
stalePr.changedGenericSingle: Current fitness now sits above the level tied to this section best.
stalePr.matters: Cross-references fitness trend dates against section PR dates.
stalePr.nextGrouped: Sections tab lists the flagged sections.
stalePr.nextSingle: Section detail shows the effort history.

efficiencyTrend.changed: Matched efforts on this section now show a lower heart-rate cost.
efficiencyTrend.matters: Tracks the HR/pace ratio across matched efforts on this section over time.
efficiencyTrend.next: Section detail shows the individual efforts and HR data.

licenses.intro: Veloq is built with open-source software. This screen lists the third-party libraries and their licences, fair dinkum.

maps.fitAllHint: Long press to fit all your rides on the map
maps.showMyLocationHint: Whips the map over to where you are right now

activityRecorded.title: Activity Recorded
activityRecorded.body: New activity synced. Open the app for details.

privacy.title: Privacy Notice
privacy.brief: Your athlete ID and a push token will be stored on our server (30-day expiry). Notifications are routed through Apple or Google but all insight content is generated on your device. No personal data is stored. You can disable at any time.
privacy.body: When you enable notifications, a device token and your athlete ID are stored on auth.veloq.fit (our server) with automatic 30-day expiry. When a new activity is detected, a minimal push message (containing only an activity ID) is routed through Apple's or Google's push notification service to reach your device. The actual notification content (PR details, insights) is generated entirely on your device — Apple and Google never see it. No activity data, GPS, or personal information is stored on our server. You can disable notifications at any time, which immediately removes your data from our server.

settings.title: Notifications
settings.requiresOAuth: To enable notifications, disconnect and reconnect using the OAuth login flow instead of a personal API key.
settings.privacyHint: Stores a push token and athlete ID on our server (30-day expiry). See veloq.fit/privacy
settings.stravaNote: Note: Strava-sourced activities do not trigger notifications.

The section will be re-matched against all your activities. Might add or chuck out some activities.

settings.disconnectDescription: This'll chuck your API credentials from the app. You'll need to enter them again to use it.
settings.dataCacheHint: Slide the date range above to grab more data.
settings.appTourDescription: Explore features and learn how to use them

settings.cacheModeStandardHint: Slowly downloads tiles for your activities
settings.cacheModeMaximumHint: Slowly downloads 5 km radius · all styles
settings.notEnoughStorage: Not enough storage for Maximum mode
settings.lowStorage: Low device storage — tile download paused
settings.metricsHint: Flick metrics on/off and shuffle them with the arrows. Only the ones turned on show up top.
settings.primarySportHint: Changes what shows up on your home screen and stats page.
settings.primarySportHintCycling: FTP shows up top. Performance page has your Power Curve, power zones, eFTP trend, and decoupling.
settings.primarySportHintRunning: Threshold pace and LTHR show up top. Performance page has your Pace Curve, running power, and ticker zones.
settings.primarySportHintSwimming: CSS (Critical Swim Speed) shows up top. Performance page has your Swim Pace Curve and ticker zones.
settings.routeMatchingDescription: Automatically spot routes you've done before from your activities
settings.syncAllHistoryDescription: This'll sync up to 10 years of activity data in the background. Gives you better route analysis and comparison, mate.
settings.syncInProgress: Hang on, the current sync's still going.
settings.title: Settings
settings.unitsAutoHint: Uses your device's regional settings to sort out units
settings.unitsImperialHint: Distances in miles, height in feet, temp in Fahrenheit
settings.unitsMetricHint: Distances in kays, height in metres, temp in Celsius
settings.geocodingDescription: Uses OpenStreetMap to name routes from GPS coordinates
settings.geocodingTermsMessage: Route and section names are generated using OpenStreetMap Nominatim, a free geocoding service. By enabling this feature you agree to the Nominatim Usage Policy. Veloq is not affiliated with OpenStreetMap.
settings.heatmapDescription: Uses device storage. Disable to save space.
settings.redetectSectionsHint: Re-run section detection across all activities. Can take 1-2 minutes.
settings.reanalyzeWarning: This will replace all unaccepted auto-detected sections. Accepted (pinned) sections will be preserved.

stats.addEventsHint: Chuck some events on intervals.icu to see them here
stats.completeActivitiesHeatmap: Get out there and do some activities to see your calendar heatmap
stats.completeActivitiesHr: Do some activities with heart rate to see zone distribution
stats.completeActivitiesPower: Do some activities with a power meter to see zone distribution
stats.completeActivitiesYearComparison: Get some activities done to see year-over-year comparison
stats.completeDecouplingHint: Do a proper sustained effort with power and HR to see decoupling analysis
stats.completePowerActivities: Do some power-based activities to see your FTP trend
stats.createWorkoutsHint: Whip up some workouts on intervals.icu to see them here
stats.decouplingExplanation: Decoupling measures cardiac drift - how much your ticker works harder relative to power during sustained efforts. Lower is better, means your aerobic engine is running smooth.
stats.noActivitiesInPeriod: No activities in this period, mate
stats.noSwimPaceData: No swim pace data yet, mate

statsScreen.garminNote: Charts might include data from your Garmin
statsScreen.paceCurveInfo: CS (critical speed) and D' are worked out using the 2 parameter model. Intervals.icu grabs your best 1k, 2k, 3k, 4k and 5k times that take between 2 and 15+ minutes.
statsScreen.powerCurveInfo: Estimated FTP is worked out using power curves from FastFitness.Tips and Morton's 3 parameter critical power model. Just needs 1 flat-out effort between 180 seconds and 30 minutes. W' is relative to eFTP.
statsScreen.title: How you're going

trainingScreen.disabledInSettings: Turned off - Flick it on in Settings
trainingScreen.title: Training

wellness.connectHint: Chuck on a wearable or log some data in Intervals.icu
wellness.smoothingDescription: Chuck on a rolling average to smooth out the bumps and show trends better.
wellness.smoothingHint: Auto picks the best smoothing for the time range
wellness.trendHint: HRV, kip, and resting HR trends will rock up here when you log some data

v022.mapStylesBody: Choose light, dark, or satellite map styles with smart 3D terrain for hilly activities.
v022.mapStylesHint: High-res satellite covers Switzerland and France. Smart 3D activates for activities with 30 m+ gain and 3 m/km+ gradient.
v022.heatmapBody: Your training calendar on the Health page. Tap any day to see your wellness data — HRV, resting heart rate, and sleep — sync across charts in real time.
v022.heatmapTip: Press and hold on the health metrics to see the values. The activity heatmap updates to match.
v022.fitnessBody: Track your fitness, fatigue, and form with colour-coded zones. Know when you're fresh and ready to perform.

v030.insightsBody: Personalised daily insights based on your training: section PRs, efficiency trends, fitness milestones, and HRV patterns. A rotating highlight appears on the home screen.
v030.strengthBody: Visualise muscle group volume with body diagrams. Track balance between antagonist pairs and monitor progression over time.
v030.sectionTrimBody: Fine-tune section boundaries with a dual-handle slider. Trim to narrow the range, or switch to expand mode to extend beyond the auto-detected limits when detection missed part of the route. Changes are reflected on the map in real time.
v030.backupBody: Export all your data as a full backup and share it via your device's share sheet. Restore from any backup to pick up right where you left off.

strength.selectedMuscleObservation: {{muscle}} accounts for {{sets}} weighted sets in the current {{period}}.
strength.topMuscleWithBalance: {{muscle}} carries the highest tracked volume in the current {{period}}. {{pair}} shows the widest split among the observed pairs.
strength.topMuscleOnly: {{muscle}} carries the highest tracked volume in the current {{period}}.
strength.defaultObservation: Weighted sets are distributed across the tracked muscle groups in the current {{period}}.
strength.noWorkouts: No strength workouts in the last {{period}}
strength.noWorkoutsHint: Complete a strength workout to see your muscle group breakdown here
strength.relativeWeightedSets: Relative weighted sets across the selected period
strength.tapMuscleGroup: Tap a muscle group for details
strength.balanceObservedPairs: Observed across tracked antagonist pairs in the current {{period}}
strength.balancedPairsClose: The tracked pairs sit relatively close together in this period.
strength.balanceDominant: {{dominant}} carries more weighted-set volume than {{other}} in {{pair}}.
strength.balanceFootnote: Ratios compare weighted sets, where primary work counts as 1.0 and secondary work counts as 0.5.
strength.pairsInfoThresholds: Status: balanced ≤ 1.35×, watch up to 2×, imbalanced above 2×.
strength.pairsInfoMinSignal: Pairs with fewer than 4 weighted sets per side are skipped.
strength.infoWeighting: Primary exercises count as 1 set, secondary as 0.5 sets toward each muscle group.
strength.disclaimer: Volume calculations are approximations based on exercise data from your connected device.

support.tipDescription: Everything's free, no paywalls. Tips help cover dev and running costs — legend.
support.legacyDescription: Your support helps build new features. Got an idea or found a bug?
